decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing cards like Snap, Ephemerate, and Ghostly Flicker to generate value and card advantage. The deck uses God-Pharaoh's Faithful and Lórien Revealed to gain life and stall the game while setting up its win condition. With a focus on flickering value creatures like Mulldrifter and Sea Gate Oracle, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent while countering key spells with Negate and finishing the game with Meeting of Minds or Glorious Gale. The deck also includes some land recursion with Archaeomancer and Mortuary Mire for added utility.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing counterspells, removal spells, and faerie creatures to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to maintain card advantage through card draw effects like Brainstorm and Lórien Revealed, while also generating tempo advantage with cards like Ninja of the Deep Hours. By efficiently controlling the board and using card advantage to outvalue the opponent, the deck aims to establish dominance and ultimately win the game through attrition.

The main goal of this deck is to control the game by countering and removing opponent's threats while drawing cards and generating value through cards like Augur of Bolas and Ninja of the Deep Hours. Utilizing cards like Spellstutter Sprite and Lórien Revealed to disrupt opponent's game plan, while also applying pressure with evasive creatures like Faerie Seer and Ninja of the Deep Hours. The deck can also generate card advantage and close out the game with cards like Thorn of the Black Rose and Murmuring Mystic. The mana base supports the deck's strategy by providing the necessary resources to keep control of the game.

The main idea of this deck is to aggressively control the game by maximizing card draw and utilizing cheap spells to quickly establish board presence and disrupt the opponent. By efficiently filling up the graveyard with spells like Thought Scour and Mental Note, the deck can cast powerful creatures like Cryptic Serpent and Tolarian Terror for little to no cost. Counterspells and disruption spells like Dispel and Deem Inferior help protect the board and maintain tempo, while cards like Lórien Revealed and Sleep of the Dead provide additional card advantage and control options.

The main focus of this deck is on using a combination of control spells and gate creatures to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up a defensive position. Prismatic Strands and Malevolent Rumble provide protection and removal, while Bitter Reunion and Pursue the Past allow for card advantage. Lightning Bolt offers direct damage, Hawkeye's Bow provides reach, and Seeker of Skybreak offers versatility. The gate creatures like Shattered Acolyte and Sneaky Snacker provide bodies on the board, while Manor Gate and Basilisk Gate offer mana fixing and extra benefits. The deck aims to control the game until it can overwhelm the opponent.

The main strategy of this Pauper 1 vs 1 Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's resources while also maintaining card advantage. The deck utilizes a combination of removal spells like Snuff Out and board wipes like Suffocating Fumes to deal with threats, along with counterspells like Counterspell and Spell Pierce to prevent key spells from resolving. Card draw and filtering are provided by Brainstorm, Thought Scour, and Mental Note. The win condition is to outvalue the opponent with efficient threats like Gurmag Angler and Murmuring Mystic while maintaining control of the game.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with aggressive red creatures and burn spells. By utilizing efficient creatures like Goblin Bushwhacker and Burning-Tree Emissary, along with removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Blast, the deck aims to push damage through early and often. Support cards like Wrenn's Resolve and Rally at the Hornburg provide additional combat tricks and board presence, while Experimental Synthesizer helps to generate card advantage. Overall, the deck is designed to apply pressure and finish the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

This Pauper 1 vs 1 Control - Ephemerate deck focuses on utilizing Ephemerate to blink key creatures like Mulldrifter and Archaeomancer, allowing for card advantage and spell recursion. The deck includes a variety of counter spells and removal spells to control the board while also incorporating card draw and scrying effects to maintain consistency. The deck aims to outlast opponents by efficiently managing resources and leveraging the synergies between blink effects and powerful ETB abilities.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy using a combination of removal spells and counterspells, while also generating card advantage through draw effects. The aim is to control the pace of the game, while also maintaining a mana advantage with the ramp spells and land cards. By outlasting the opponent and slowly building up resources, the deck can eventually overwhelm them with powerful threats like Lórien Revealed and Malevolent Rumble. The key is to stay patient and wait for the right moment to strike.

This deck focuses on using cheap and efficient creatures and removal spells to apply pressure to the opponent early in the game. The deck also utilizes cards that can provide card advantage and mana fixing to ensure a consistent and powerful board presence. The synergy between the creatures and spells allows for a fast and aggressive playstyle, while also providing some flexibility and sustainability for longer games. The deck's combination of creatures, removal, and utility cards create a well-rounded approach to winning the game through consistent damage and strategic advantage.

The main concept of this deck is to assemble a board of Artifact creatures quickly and efficiently, while also using cards like Galvanic Blast for direct damage. The deck utilizes cards like Myr Enforcer and Refurbished Familiar to establish a strong presence on the board, while also generating card advantage through cards like Ichor Wellspring and Thoughtcast. It also includes cards like Makeshift Munitions and Krark-Clan Shaman for additional damage and disruption. The dual lands and mana ramp provided by cards like Mistvault Bridge and Blood Fountain help with consistency and mana fixing.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing Ephemerate to repeatedly trigger powerful enters-the-battlefield effects on creatures like Mulldrifter and Archaeomancer. The deck includes a mix of card draw, countermagic, and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up its own board presence. The inclusion of snow-covered lands allows for additional synergies and flexibility in mana fixing. The goal is to outvalue the opponent over the course of the game and secure victory through incremental advantage and powerful creature interactions.

The primary strategy of this deck revolves around utilizing removal spells and sacrificing creatures to control the board while generating value through card draw and token generation. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building up its own resources to outlast and outvalue the opponent. With a mix of removal, sacrifice outlets, and card advantage engines, the Golgari/Jund Garden deck seeks to establish control over the game and grind out a win through attrition.

The deck focuses on using gates as a way to control the game, with a mixture of removal spells and creatures to disrupt the opponent's strategy. It aims to stall the game with defensive plays until it can establish a board presence and finish off the opponent with powerful attackers. The use of gates allows for mana fixing and ramp, while the control elements help to secure victory by outlasting the opponent. The deck also utilizes a combination of card draw and graveyard recursion to maintain consistency and resilience throughout the match.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt your opponent's game plan while efficiently controlling the board with counterspells and bounce spells. By utilizing cheap cantrip spells to dig for answers and powerful threats like Cryptic Serpent and Murmuring Mystic, this Mono Blue Terror deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver your opponent. The key strategy is to consistently draw and play threats while keeping the opponent's threats at bay with counter magic. Through a combination of card advantage and tempo plays, this deck seeks to control the game and emerge victorious.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on countering opponent's spells, removing threats, and gaining card advantage through card draw and evasion creatures like Ninjas. It aims to control the game tempo and outvalue the opponent by efficiently using removal and counterspells. The deck seeks to disrupt the opponent's game plan while generating card advantage and maintaining board presence through its versatile creatures and removal spells. Additionally, it utilizes lands that offer additional utility and card advantage to further enhance its control gameplay.
